situational_context: Extended low-training-load period: chronic load at 18 (~1% of 2023 historical peak of 1608). Activity limited to e-bike commuting (load 7-15/session) with one structured Zwift session (load 15). HRV trending upward over last 7d (33→42 overnight avg), suggesting improving recovery capacity or reduced physiological stress. RHR stable at 52-53 bpm. Sleep adequate (7.3h avg over 28d) but variable (5.9-8.3h range), with cohabitant-driven fragmentation likely contributing to low nights. Weight stable at ~107 kg across 2 readings in 28d. Readiness flags consistently 'high' — autonomic system is not stressed despite low fitness. Training readiness scores 75-95 indicate capacity for load increase. Garmin VO2max running estimate: 40.9. Pattern consistent with life-constrained detraining (infant cohabitant) rather than injury or illness.

recommendations.text: Begin structured rebuild: add 2-3 low-intensity aerobic sessions (Z1-Z2) per week alongside commute rides
recommendations.measurable: Acute load next week ≥20 with ≥2 dedicated training sessions

recommendations.text: Maintain sleep consistency — target ≥7h on all nights, especially on training days
Log body composition measurement with full Withings scan (fat%, muscle mass) to establish rebuild baseline
recommendations.measurable: ≥1 full-body Withings measurement logged next week

markers_to_watch.threshold: Sustaining >35 ms would confirm positive recovery trajectory
markers_to_watch.reason: Upward HRV trend suggests readiness for load increase; reversal would indicate external stressor

markers_to_watch.threshold: Target gradual increase toward 50+ over next 4 weeks
markers_to_watch.reason: Currently at 1% of historical peak — any structured training will move the needle
